title: SDK Contract Fixture
description: Schema for language-agnostic SDK contract fixtures consumed by Java JsonFixtureContractTest and Python contract runner. Lane P (drift guard).

config.description: SDK boot config (tenantId / workerCode / ...). Schema intentionally open.

state.description: Optional simulated SDK state (in-flight tasks, FSM state, etc).

path.description: HTTP path (for channel=http); kebab/slash form, must start with /internal/.

body.description: Outgoing request body or incoming kafka payload.

responseBody.description: Platform response payload (null for kafka receive).

sdkExpectedAction.description: Human-readable description of behavior the SDK MUST exhibit.

expect.description: Machine-assertable expectation (BYO conformance). Optional but RECOMMENDED: every language runner asserts these discrete fields identically, instead of parsing the human-readable sdkExpectedAction prose. Closed vocabulary — adding a field requires a schema bump so expectations themselves cannot drift.

action.description: Primary SDK action the response/message must trigger.

retry.description: Whether the SDK retries this call.

retryBackoffMs.description: Expected sleep sequence between attempts, e.g. [200,400,800].

failFast.description: Mark dispatcher fatal and stop accepting tasks.

fsmTransition.description: Target worker FSM state, or null when unchanged.

kafka.description: Required Kafka consumer action. 'drop-message' = skip WITHOUT committing offset (redeliverable: paused-type / schema-reject / foreign-tenant). 'commit-skip' = skip AND commit offset to advance past an unrecoverable poison record (decode/parse error).

heartbeatNextIntervalMs.description: Effective next heartbeat interval after applying nextHeartbeatHint.

effectiveMaxConcurrent.description: Effective local concurrency cap after applying a heartbeat desiredMaxConcurrent directive (§2.1 dynamic backpressure). Absent directive → unconstrained (field omitted).

reportFailure.description: Whether the SDK reports a task FAILURE for this scenario.

withinMs.description: Hard wall-clock bound the action must complete within (e.g. stop timeout).

requestBodyIncludes.description: Request-side assertion: the SDK's OUTGOING request body (the one it would construct for this call) must DEEP-CONTAIN every key/value here (subset match — extra keys allowed). Each language runner builds the outgoing request from given.config/given.state via its decision core and asserts containment. Guards report field-name red-lines (outputs/success:bool) and partitionInvocationId pass-through.

requestBodyExcludes.description: Request-side assertion: the SDK's OUTGOING request body MUST NOT contain any of these top-level keys (e.g. legacy mis-names output/errorClass/errorMessage/status, or apiKey which belongs in a header).

requestHeaders.description: Request-side assertion: the SDK's OUTGOING request headers must contain each key; the value is treated as a regex the actual header value must fully match (e.g. Idempotency-Key '^[a-z]+-[0-9a-f-]{8,}$'). Use to lock the apiKey→header rule and per-call idempotency-key uniqueness.

schemaAccept.description: Kafka-only: whether the SDK ACCEPTS this dispatch message given its schemaVersion (§A). true = process; false = reject and do NOT commit offset (unknown major v3+).
